name: Wheat & Meat Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, bring the presoaked wheat and water used for soaking to a boil. Cover and cook for 1 hour.
- If necessary, add more water as the kernels cook.
2. Melt the suet or lard in a large heavy pot over medium-high heat. Remove rendered suet pieces from the pot.
3. Add the onions to the pot and cook until they are translucent.
4. Combine the ground beef with the ground chile, garlic, oregano, cumin, and salt to taste.
5. Add this beef-and-spice mixture to the pot with the onions.
6. Break up any lumps with a fork and stir occasionally until the meat is evenly browned.
7. Stir in the Chile caribe, green chiles, tomato paste, and tomato juice.
8. Drain the wheat, reserving some liquid. Stir the kernels into the mixture.
9. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer uncovered for 1 hour.
- If the chili becomes too dry, add some of the reserved liquid from the wheat cooking.
10. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
